key_topics: Fractions: multiplying fractions and mixed numbers
Dividing unit fractions and whole numbers
Decimals: all four operations with decimals to thousandths
Place value: understanding through 1,000,000,000
Order of operations: PEMDAS with multiple steps
Introduction to integers: positive and negative on a number line
Coordinate plane: all four quadrants, graphing ordered pairs
Patterns and functions: input/output tables
Volume: rectangular prisms (V = l × w × h)
Measurement conversions: customary and metric
Geometry: classifying polygons, triangles, quadrilaterals
competences: Multiply and divide fractions and mixed numbers
Perform all four operations with decimals accurately
Graph points in all four quadrants of the coordinate plane
typical_mistakes: PEMDAS errors: performing addition before multiplication
Inversion error in division of fractions (not flipping divisor)
Decimal multiplication: misplacing the decimal in the product
Confusing x and y axes when graphing coordinates

key_topics: Reading: classic literature, myths and legends from world cultures
Literary analysis: author's purpose, point of view, rhetoric
Comprehension strategies: visualizing, questioning, synthesising
Informational text: primary and secondary sources
Evaluating arguments: identifying logical fallacies
Writing: argumentative essays with evidence and counterargument
Research paper: MLA format, bibliography, in-text citation
Grammar: gerunds, infinitives, participial phrases
Advanced vocabulary: etymology, connotation vs. denotation
Speaking: oral reports, Socratic seminars
competences: Write argumentative essays with counterclaims and rebuttals
Complete a structured research paper with citations
Use participial phrases and gerunds correctly in writing
typical_mistakes: Circular reasoning in arguments (using the claim as evidence)
Not integrating quotes (dropping them without introduction)

key_topics: The Enlightenment: Locke, Rousseau, Voltaire, Montesquieu
American Revolution: detailed study of key battles and figures
The Constitution: Articles, Amendments, Federalist Papers
19th Century America: Industrial Revolution, immigration
Abolition movement: Frederick Douglass, Harriet Tubman, Underground Railroad
The Civil War: detailed study — Gettysburg, Sherman's March
World Geography: Asia — physical and political features
Latin America: geography, history, independence movements
competences: Explain Enlightenment ideas and their influence on the Constitution
Analyse primary sources from the Civil War era
Describe the Industrial Revolution's impact on American society
Locate and describe physical and political features of Asia
typical_mistakes: Not understanding how Enlightenment ideas influenced the founders

key_topics: Life Science: cells — mitosis, DNA basics
Genetics: traits, dominant and recessive genes, Punnett squares
Evolution: natural selection, fossil evidence, adaptation
Earth Science: plate tectonics, earthquakes, volcanoes
Atmosphere: layers, weather patterns, climate change basics
Physical Science: chemistry — atoms, elements, compounds, mixtures
Chemical vs. physical changes
The Periodic Table: introduction to organisation
competences: Complete basic Punnett squares for monohybrid crosses
Explain plate tectonics and connect to earthquakes/volcanoes
Distinguish physical from chemical changes with examples
Explain natural selection using a real or hypothetical example
typical_mistakes: Punnett square errors (not filling all four boxes)
